rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component implementation scope when binding to subcomponent ports or implementation-specific logic.
Ensure contracts in component type scope reference only visible features, not subcomponent paths.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL scope boundaries: type-level annexes must use only externally visible features; implementation-level annexes can bind internal subcomponents.
When propagating state across components, express dependencies through visible input/output ports, not internal paths.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` for AGREE blocks; place guarantees and equations in the scope that owns the referenced features.
Implementation-level bindings (e.g., assign) require the target to be a visible output port of the implementation.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports or internal signals in component type annexes — this breaks scope encapsulation.
Avoid assuming implementation details (e.g., internal variable names) in type-level contracts.
irrelevant_topics: Assign statement usage for state updates
